title: Allergic Rhinitis
description: Allergic Rhinitis app - helps in understanding the disease condition through ANIMATED VIDEOS. This presentation discusses the functioning of human immune system, its role in the onset of allergic rhinitis, diagnosis and treatment methods, along with guidelines to prevent and manage rhinitis.

title: Allergic Rhinitis/ Hay Fever
description: An allergy is an inappropriate response by the body's immune system to a substance that is normally harmful. The immune system is a highly complex defense mechanism that helps us to fight infections. Allergic rhinitis/ hay fever is an inflammation inside the nose caused by an allergen such as pollen, foods like ( shellfish, milk, soya, strawberries, chocolate, peanuts, walnuts, and other tree nuts, fish, eggs and wheat) dust , molds, or flakes of skin from certain animals, animal hair, certain metals like nickel, some cosmetics, lanolin, dust mites, insect venom, some common drugs ( such as penicillin and aspirin) some food additives ( such as benzoic acid and sulfur dioxide), animal dander and the chemicals found in soap, washing powder, cleaning supplies, and many other chemicals.
descriptionHTML: An allergy is an inappropriate response by the body's immune system to a substance that is normally harmful. summary: Is an inflammation inside the nose caused by an allergen.
developerAddress: 55 Choa Chu Kang Loop #04-23 The Warren Singapore 6896684

title: MASK-air
description:
The best application for monitoring allergic rhinitis - also called  hay fever - according to health care professionals (doctors, pharmacists) and patients. The MASK-air application has been developed by internationally-renowned allergists and has been scientifically validated.

Take immediate control of your rhinitis:
⁻ by becoming a real actor of your own health 
⁻ by preventing new crises through knowing your symptoms better and avoiding aggravation (asthma)
⁻ by having a personalized day-to-day follow-up
⁻ by measuring the effectiveness of the treatment and its possible adaptation
⁻ by anticipating crises and their management for the following year

MASK-air is an allergic rhinitis logbook - always in your pocket, user friendly, with automatic data backup, with data analysis and visual images to enable your healthcare professional (doctor and/or pharmacist) to manage you better.

Record the symptoms of your allergic crises daily as well as the impact on your quality of life and the medications taken. This will help you and your healthcare professionals to determine the diagnosis and effective treatment for YOUR allergic rhinitis.

Many patients suffer just like you from their allergies – 20 to 30% of the world's population! Help the patient community and participate in advancing research. MASK-air is an international research project aiming to better understand this pathology, for better care. The app is deployed in more than 20 countries around the world.

Allergic rhinitis is a pathology that is not "serious" in itself but that can be troublesome on a daily basis as it can cause complications such as the inability to sleep due to symptoms, excessive fatigue, development or worsening of asthma which can have an important impact on daily life such as absence from work  or repeated absence during studies...

No identifying data are collected through the use of this application. If you accept the geolocation of your symptoms, this data will be codified on our servers and will respect your privacy. All the data are kept on a secure server in Europe. 

The n° 1 app for monitoring allergic rhinitis and asthma / Easy, informative and secure / Scientifically validated / 

Answer the questionnaire every day In less than 2 minutes  / Quick / 

Understand and manage your allergy properly / Detailed reports for you and your healthcare professionals

comments: I am happy that something like this exists, but had some trouble due to the app popping up unexpectedly, even though I instructed it to ask me questions just once in a week.
great app, love the visual scale. hopefully it helps tracking the effect of medication charges. note that your support email is bouncing.
This app just send you around in circle not helpful at all

The idea is quite nice but it has some serious usability issues. Why can you only work with the entry for the current day? What if you forgot one (or more) or realize that you made a mistake? As you can't add a comment either I guess I've to switch back to paper

title: KoRhina: Alleviate allergic rhinitis symptoms
description:
◼ Easily control KoRhina laser rhinitis treatment device connected wirelessly via Bluetooth.
◼ Provides usage history of KoRhina laser rhinitis treatment by day, week, month, and year.
◼ Check environmental information that affects rhinitis, such as weather, temperature, humidity, dust, and pollen.
◼ Personalized rhinitis prevention information using big data analysis and AI technology will be provided in the future.

◼ What is KoRhina laser rhinitis treatment device
KoRhina, a wearable smart laser irradiator, is a medical device with 650nm wavelength / 5mW output power, which treats allergic rhinitis by injecting a laser probe into the nasal cavity and emitting laser.

  ◻ Able to use conveniently anytime, anywhere with ultra-compact size (50x65x17mm, 30g or less)
  ◻ Improved treatment effect by laser diffusion
  ◻ Supports Bluetooth to connect with this app

◼ Data Providers
  ◻ Weather, pollen in Korea: KMA
  ◻ Air quality in Korea: AirKorea

◼ Required permission
  ◻  Location service is required to use Bluetooth for connecting app and KoRhina device.

◼ Mirint Co., Ltd: http://www.mirint.com

title: Rhinitis Alert
descriptionHTML:
Did you know that your nonallergic rhinitis is influenced by the weather around you?

Environmental factors, such as barometric pressure, humidity and temperature, do influence your rhinitis symptoms.

With Rhinitis Alert, you can be notified when barometric pressure, humidity and/or temperature have abrupt changes or go below/above a certain level. Also, with the symptom feedback option and 5-day history, you can relate weather events with your symptoms!

Features:
- Symptoms expected for today and tomorrow (based on your input);
- 3 day forecast of barometric pressure, humidity, temperature and general weather;
- Daily symptoms feedback tracking;
- 5 day history charts, showing temperature, barometric pressure or humidity in comparison with the symptoms feedback given;
- Optional daily notifications if temperature, barometric pressure or humidity go above/below a certain value, or have abrupt changes from one day to another.
- Completely free, no ads.

App icon and banner designed by Rita Nobre.
rita_nobre@outlook.com
summary: Rhinitis Alert sends you notifications when abrupt weather changes occur.
recentChanges:
☼ Bug fixes;
☼ General UX improvements;
☼ Minor layout fixes.
comments: I just installed the app and it looks like it would be great for me, except that the humidity here is 86 and the app says 100. If that could be fixed, I would want give it 10⭐!!
This app is so good it triggers me

Update: Thanks for adding manual location! The automatic locationing still 
doesn't work for me, but now I can use the app with the manual entry. Would 
be great to choose units of measurement for pressure, too. Prior Review: No 
data. Everything shows up as unknown. The idea here is great. Too bad it 
doesn't work. I even checked to make sure it had location permission 
(though I prefer manual location entry).

title: Pollen Wise - What's in your air, when and where
description:
Own Your Seasonal Allergies with the Pollen Wise App. Using a robust network of sensors around the country, Pollen Wise Provides up-to-date pollen counts to help users mitigate allergens and avoid seasonal allergy symptoms. 
 
If you have asthma, hay fever or seasonal allergies, knowing what is in TODAY's air is more valuable than knowing what was in YESTERDAY's air. Unlike your current pollen report or app, Pollen Wise connects you with timely information from new pollen sensors. They create reports every few minutes for an hourly average. For the first time, this kind of information helps you make decisions about your exposure to pollen.
 
Ever-changing levels of pollen can make it difficult to know when you should go outside or when it's better to stay inside. For example, levels can spike on one day at 11 am, drop at 2 pm, then begin climbing again until 8 pm. On the following day those levels could be reversed, but you'd never know without the kind of details that Pollen Wise can provide you because your source for pollen information is based on predictions and yesterday's counts.
 
If you have better information, it only makes sense to use it. Pollen Wise: What's in your air, when and where...

recentChanges:
Pollen Wise has hit version 2.0! This version lays the groundwork for lots of exciting features. Major changes in this update:
Create an account with us to continue to add to your journal! Having an account means you can access your journal entries and future personalized features on any platform our app is offered on.
We have also found and fixed a whole host of bugs.
comments: Best allergy/asthma tracking app I've tried! Unlike many similar apps, this one does NOT require you to sign up for an account. I love the detailed hourly information. Things that would be cool to see added: 1.) Widgets. Preferably a 1x1, but a few size options would be nice. 2.) Ability to add custom symptoms/more symptom options. The specific symptoms I'd like to add: itchy nose, itchy throat, headache, dizziness.

Wow! This is the best pollen/allergy app I've ever used! Downloaded a few others before this because they had more users, but this one is hands down the most accurate and useful. Really informative and pleasing user interface as well! Thank you for making this app. Also just wanted to suggest a way to view the previous 24hrs of data? It would be helpful to see that data

I like this app, but really wish there was a sensor in my area. The closest one is 150 miles away. Edit #1 because since I wrote this, my nearest sensor is now 75 miles away. Also, liking the journal for tracking symptoms. Could use the option of "sinus headache" under symptoms too. Edit #2, back down to 3 stars. I lost my local station and now the forecasts don't help.

This app is absolutely great for anyone suffering from hay fever. I have just one teeny weeny problem. The nearest sensor to my location is a astonishing 7075 miles away. I don't think a sensor that far away will be able to provide an accurate description of the amount of pollen in my area. Unless the producers tackle this issue, this app just cannot work for me, though it is not the producers' fault. If you live relatively close to a sensor, I would totally recommend.

Great app and proff that it works..every day when the weed pollen was high my i couldn't stop sneezing with my eyes and noses running absolutely unbearable but luckily enough when I take my antihistamine after a while I'd be fine. Keep up the good work.

title: Seasonal Allergies
description:
Seasonal allergies, sometimes called "hay fever" or seasonal allergic rhinitis, are allergy symptoms that happen during certain times of the year, usually when outdoor molds release their spores, and trees, grasses, and weeds release tiny pollen particles into the air to fertilize other plants.
The immune systems of people who are allergic to mold spores or pollen treat these particles (called allergens) as invaders and release chemicals, including histamine, into the bloodstream to defend against them. It's the release of these chemicals that causes allergy symptoms.
People can be allergic to one or more types of pollen or mold. The type someone is allergic to determines when symptoms happen. For example, in the mid-Atlantic states, tree pollination is February through May, grass pollen runs from May through June, and weed pollen is from August through October — so kids with these allergies are likely to have increased symptoms at those times. Mold spores tend to peak midsummer through the fall, depending on location.
Even kids who have never had seasonal allergies in years past can develop them. Seasonal allergies can start at almost any age, though they usually develop by the time someone is 10 years old and reach their peak in the early twenties, with symptoms often disappearing later in adulthood.
Seasonal allergies examples: Spring Allergies, Summer Allergies, Fall Allergies, Winter Allergies, Hay Fever, Pollen Allergies, Mold Allergy, Dust Allergy, Dog Allergy, Cat Allergy, allergy and asthma and allergic reaction rash.
Seasonal allergies App Features:
•	What is Seasonal Allergies? 
•	Seasonal Allergies Symptoms.
•	How to Control Your Seasonal Allergies.
•	Natural Seasonal Allergy Home Remedies.
•	Seasonal Allergies Causes.
•	Seasonal Allergies Diagnosis.
•	Seasonal Allergies Treatment.

title: Hay Fever Allergy
description: Allergic rhinitis, also known as hay fever, is a type of inflammation in the nose which occurs when the immune system overreacts to allergens in the air. Signs and symptoms include a runny or stuffy nose, sneezing, red, itchy, and watery eyes, and swelling around the eyes. The fluid from the nose is usually clear. Symptom onset is often within minutes following exposure and they can affect sleep, the ability to work, and the ability to concentrate at school. Those whose symptoms are due to pollen typically develop symptoms during specific times of the year. Many people with allergic rhinitis also have asthma, allergic conjunctivitis, or atopic dermatitis.

Symptom onset is often within minutes following exposure and they can affect sleep, the ability to work, and the ability to concentrate at school. Those whose symptoms are due to pollen typically develop symptoms during specific times of the year. Many people with allergic rhinitis also have asthma, allergic conjunctivitis, or atopic dermatitis.
summary: Hay fever is type of inflammation in the nose which occurs.

title: TOTAAL - Asthma and Allergies
description:
Tropical Outdoor Triggers of Asthma and Allergies (TOTAAL ) application is a free app that provides de levels of pollen and fungal spores for the benefit of allergy sufferers. TOTAAL app is for individual with allergies specifically fungal spores that triggers allergies of the eyes (allergic conjunctivitis), the nose (allergic rhinitis), the skin (atopic dermatitis/eczema and or hives) and those with asthma. This app will help you correlate your allergies and asthma with the local fungal counts.
 
TOTAAL app allows you to see real pollen and fungal spore counts in San Juan, PR. TOTAAL app has been developed by Dr. Benjamin Bolaños, Director an d founder of the San Juan, PR AAAAI Station of the Medical Sciences Campus, of the University of Puerto Rico. . Dr. Bolaños is an Associate Professor of the Department of Microbiology of the Medical Sciences Campus, University of Puerto Rico. He has been involved in clinical studies on the role of fungal spores as allergens and purification of tropical fungal allergens. 

Any use of this information provided by the app is for personal use only.
 
The TOTAAL app are the property of the San Juan Station of the Medical Sciences Campus. Any use, copying, reproducing or re-publishing of these these materials is prohibited without the prior written approval of Benjamin Bolaños and the Medical Sciences Campus, University of Puerto Rico.

comments: I would absolutely love this app if it was more specific. "Tree pollen high" great but what type of tree? And what are the counts? Its too generalized for liking. But I love how simple it is! And I wish there was an option to have more than 1 location also.
just downloaded it and it doesn't let me put in my location. I dont even know where its getting its information.
